format.recording_location: Bellman office studio, Dubai (in-person), remote as backup depending on guest availability and Dubai situation

editorial_philosophy.core_principle: Nobody cares about you. The audience is the hero. Every conversation must deliver actionable value the viewer can apply today.
editorial_philosophy.positioning: Focuses on projects and companies built with intention and purpose, aiming to create meaningful impact rather than short-term financial returns. Counterbalances the startup ecosystem's obsession with rapid growth and monetization.
editorial_philosophy.honesty_angle: Intentionally avoids presenting founders as flawless experts. Emphasizes the realities of building: uncertainty, experimentation, learning through experience.
editorial_philosophy.host_approach: Ayax participates as a builder and storyteller, not an industry authority. Asks questions that explore reasoning, decisions, and challenges behind the work.
editorial_philosophy.what_they_want: Actionable takeaways from founders building with purpose. What can I learn from your story that I can apply to my business today?
editorial_philosophy.what_they_dont_want: Ego-driven storytelling, generic founder origin stories, hustle culture, 'get rich with AI' narratives, overnight success stories
editorial_philosophy.content_strategy: Episodes structured for short-form extraction. Ayax will prepare questions specifically designed to trigger strong standalone clips. Expects quick iterations, format explorations, and access to raw material/editing projects.
editorial_philosophy.guest_prep_note: Guests will be briefed: don't talk about yourself, focus on what the audience can learn. 'We are not the heroes in our story.'

content_pillars.name: Purpose & Impact
content_pillars.description: What problems are they solving and why it matters

content_pillars.name: The Reality of Building
content_pillars.description: Honest conversations about the journey: uncertainty, experimentation, learning through failure

content_pillars.name: Idea to Adoption
content_pillars.description: How to make stories reach the world. The role of communication and narrative in innovation.

content_pillars.name: Builders & Ecosystems
content_pillars.description: Creating environments where impact can grow. Community, infrastructure, support systems.

client.name: Ayax Bader

links.community: 24six9 (chapters in Dubai, Riyadh, Athens, and more)
links.news_platform: Tech Revolt (media partner access to events)
links.investment_arm: Actual VC (Ahoy's deep tech investment arm)

team.role: Team member, to be included on all comms

team.name: Jamil
team.role: Key collaborator, extensive network, source of guest suggestions. Will announce the show through his LinkedIn as a project he co-runs.

notable_background: Audiovisual industry background (editing, post-production, After Effects)
Runs a branding agency (branding workshops, storytelling frameworks)
Runs a video production company with 4 in-house video editors
Previously ran a podcast (understands the medium deeply)
Content creator on personal accounts, content-first mindset
NOT a founder who got into content, but a creator who became a founder

company.name: Bellman Media
company.positioning: Branding agency and video production company. Part of the Ahoy ecosystem which builds software tools for developers to lower the barrier of entry to high tech globally.

ahoy_context.what_ahoy_does: Creates software tools for developers to lower the barrier of entry to high tech, promoting geography-agnostic Deep Tech development
ahoy_context.ahoy_ventures: Actual VC, deep tech investment arm working with startups
ahoy_context.media_ecosystem: Building a media ecosystem around Ahoy: Tech Revolt (news publication) + events IP + Build to Matter (podcast)

goals.runway: 6 months funded by Ahoy, review point at end of Month 3
primary_objectives: Grow a relevant channel in the startup niche to leverage exposure, influence and grow Bellman's brand equity
Promote entrepreneurship and impactful projects/founders to leverage relationships
Establish Ahoy's USP: lowering barrier of entry, enabling geography-agnostic innovation
Boost Ahoy Ventures / Actual VC and potential partners through exposure
Get branding and video work for Bellman through Ayax's positioning
goals.success_definition: Consistent output and fast iteration, not chasing virality. Every episode is a chance to learn what formats, topics, and angles land best, then double down on what performs.
success_criteria_any_one_of: A valuable partnership for Ahoy (credibility to justify continued funding)
Branding work for Bellman (leverage podcast conversations into branding workshop sales)
Sponsorship that covers production costs (self-sustaining model)

persona_a.who: Founders in the thick of it: people building technology with real-world impact, still in the messy stages of figuring things out
persona_a.description: Working on meaningful problems, navigating uncertainty, making hard decisions daily

persona_b.who: Future founders: aspiring builders not yet started but on the path
persona_b.roles: Engineers, designers, startup employees, operators who want to build
persona_b.description: Learning, watching, getting ready to start their own thing

mindset: Building is hard and uncertain, and that's normal
Ideas only matter if they reach adoption
Ecosystems improve when builders challenge each other
Communication and narrative matter as much as the product
pain_points: Toxic startup content: hustle culture, 'get rich with AI,' overnight success stories
Fake expertise everywhere: suddenly everyone is an expert, in reality nobody has a clue
Misaligned incentives: too many people chasing quick returns instead of solving meaningful problems

guest_criteria.primary_profile: Early to mid-stage founders building in areas like climate, health, infrastructure, fintech, education, dev tools, AI with real applications. Thoughtful, reflective, willing to discuss uncertainty and decisions. Motivated by impact, not just fast exits.
guest_criteria.geographic_focus: Middle East / UAE region primarily, but open globally
ideal_traits: Willing to be honest about the messy reality of building
Thoughtful and reflective about decisions and uncertainty
Able to share lessons that other builders can actually use
not_looking_for: Generic SaaS/marketing automation founders with no impact angle
Pure 'we raised money' stories without substance
Hustle culture figures or 'get rich quick' personalities
Self-proclaimed experts with no real building experience
People selling courses or clout with no track record
Anyone who just wants to promote themselves
sourcing_channels: Jamil (key collaborator, huge network in the region)
Actual VC portfolio companies (Ahoy's investment arm, natural pipeline)
24six9 community (founder chapters in Dubai, Riyadh, Athens, and more)
Tech Revolt (Bellman's news platform, media partner access to regional events)
Nalu outreach (research and direct outreach via Airtable)

kpis.measurement_philosophy: First priority is consistent output. Second is identifying what formats, topics, and angles perform best. Third is doing more of what works. Speed of iteration matters more than any single metric.
open_questions: What does a qualified lead look like? Bellman branding services, Ahoy partnership, or both?
What kind of booked call? Bellman sales conversation or Ahoy intro meeting?
Is there a services page, booking link, or partnership page to drive traffic to?
Will there be a newsletter tied to the podcast? Who builds and runs it?

nalu_handles: Full editing: long-form + short-form clip selection and editing
YouTube management (uploading, thumbnails, A/B testing with up to 6 variations)
Copywriting: carousels, newsletter, social media posts, captions
Notion workspace setup (foundational docs, content calendar, sign-off workflow)
Recording support: on-site operator + extra equipment if needed
bellman_handles: Carousel/infographic design (Nalu provides copy, Bellman designs)
Quick iterations and format explorations (content-first approach)
ownership.partnership_dynamic: Nalu brings strategic podcast experience and format expertise. Bellman brings content production knowledge and video capabilities. Ayax is a creator-turned-founder, not a founder getting into content. This is a real collaboration, not a handoff.

commercials.adjustment_reason: Bellman handling social media posting and carousel/infographic design in-house

distribution.current_audience_size: Zero across all channels, building from scratch

leverage_points.how: Founder network across Dubai, Riyadh, Athens, and more. Built-in launch audience and cross-promotion.
Ahoy's investment arm. Connections to impact startups for guests and shared promotion.
News platform with media partner access to events. Cross-promotion and guest sourcing.
Extensive connections in the founder/startup ecosystem. Will announce show as a project he co-runs.
Existing content creator audience. Primary cross-promotion channel.

distribution.outreach_domains: buildtomattermedia.com or similar, fully owned by Bellman

month_1_april.name: Pre-Production
month_1_april.focus: Build the foundation so everything is tight before go-live
setup: Outreach domains created, fully owned by Bellman
Airtable guest tracker built and shared
Notion workspace live with content calendar, foundational docs, sign-off workflow
guest_pipeline: First wave of email outreach using dedicated domains
Nalu sources initial guest list for Ayax to review and approve
Bellman side (Jamil, Ayax, 24six9, Actual VC) feeds in suggestions
Book and record at least 3 episodes as buffer
Mix of in-person and remote depending on availability
content_prep: All recorded episodes edited, clipped, packaged (long-form + 5-6 shorts each)
Full content calendar drafted in Notion with all assets ready for sign-off
Ayax and team review and approve everything before go-live
Two-week teaser window devised leading into first published episode

month_2_may.name: Go Live & Build the Base Layer
month_2_may.focus: Publish, get the word out, test what resonates, build momentum organically
publishing: Full content package per episode: long-form, 5-6 shorts, carousel copy, newsletter copy, social captions
Bellman handles design and posting; Nalu provides written content and creative direction
YouTube A/B testing on thumbnails and titles (up to 6 variations)
amplification: Jamil announces the show through LinkedIn as a project he co-runs
Ayax cross-promotes through personal channels and 24six9
Explore PR and cross-promotion with relevant Dubai/UAE networks, media outlets, and communities
iteration: Monitor performance data across YouTube, streaming platforms, and social
Track how repurposed assets perform relative to long-form
Identify which topics, guests, and pillars get the strongest response
Review whether visual identity is landing well in the wild
pipeline: Sharpen guest targeting based on learnings from first episodes

month_3_june.name: Optimise & Explore Growth Levers
month_3_june.focus: With ~6 episodes live and real data, double down on what works and explore paid media
performance_review: Full review across YouTube, Spotify, Apple, and streaming platforms
Assess repurposed asset performance across social channels
Identify top-performing content pillars and guest types
Evaluate social account growth and strongest traction points
iteration: Double down on best-performing formats, topics, and angles
Adjust content calendar and guest pipeline based on data
Refine repurposing strategy if certain asset types outperform

paid_media_option.trigger: If organic performs well we may not need it, but if we see opportunity to accelerate this is when we explore
paid_media_option.approach: Take best-performing organic creatives and run as Google search intent campaigns and in-feed YouTube ads
paid_media_option.goal: Drive qualified traffic to a clear endpoint (book a call, partnership page, sponsorship opportunity)
paid_media_option.value: Twofold: grow YouTube channel/audience through qualified traffic while driving direct response outcomes
paid_media_option.target: Break even on ad spend, meaning audience growth and brand exposure come effectively for free

review: End-of-quarter review with Ayax to assess progress against success criteria
